rationale: The paper focuses on control theory and optimization for water distribution networks using geometric programming, which is an off-domain application distinct from the user's question about robot policy learning and geometric action models in computer science. While both use the term "geometric," the underlying constructs (hydraulic flow equations vs. robot kinematics/dynamics) and domains (civil engineering vs. robotics/ML) are entirely different, satisfying the rejection rule for distinct const
